denial_letter_text:
NOTICE OF ADVERSE BENEFIT DETERMINATION

Date of Notice: 03/13/2026
Aetna Choice® POS II (employer-sponsored group health plan)
Member ID: W102288591          Group #: GRP-1289
Authorization / Reference #: PA-2026-143614
Claim #: CLM-2026-203318
Date of Service (requested): 02/21/2026
Treating provider (as submitted): Central States Medical Associates

Dear Plan Member,

This document is notice of an adverse benefit determination. We received your provider's prior authorization request on 02/23/2026 for Lacosamide add-on therapy related to Focal epilepsy, drug-resistant (G40.019). After clinical review, we are unable to approve this request.

CASE SUMMARY
Service requested: Lacosamide add-on therapy
Diagnosis (as submitted): Focal epilepsy, drug-resistant (G40.019)
Denial code: CO-197 — Precertification/authorization absent — service requires prior approval before the date of service.
Clinical policy applied: CPB 0109 under Aetna Clinical Policy Bulletin (CPB) and InterQual® acute-care criteria

EXPLANATION OF DECISION
Our review applied the criteria in CPB 0109 and your plan's Summary Plan Description (SPD), Section 4 — Medical Benefits and Utilization Management. The documentation submitted does not demonstrate that the service is medically necessary for this member at this time. Emergency imaging/treatment for Focal epilepsy, drug-resistant (G40.019) is denied for lack of prior authorization. Retroactive authorization is not granted for non-emergent services.

Specifically, the information provided does not satisfy the MCG Care Guidelines (edition not specified) and MCG Care Guidelines (edition not specified) and utilization-management requirements associated with emergency retroactive auth. The clinical notes reviewed did not include sufficient detail to confirm that plan criteria were met. The requesting provider may submit additional records, but the current file does not support approval.

This review used clinical criteria from our designated utilization-management program; the criteria document is available upon request.

Diagnosis and procedure codes used in this determination are available upon request, including plain-language descriptions, as required by applicable federal standards.

If your provider wishes to discuss this determination, they may contact our physician review line. Any discussion must occur within the timeframe described in your plan materials.

YOUR RIGHT TO INFORMATION
You may request a copy of your claim file and the clinical criteria we used in this determination. Submit your request to the address on this notice or through your plan's member portal.

Your treating physician may request a peer-to-peer discussion with an Aetna medical director before filing a written appeal.

APPEAL RIGHTS
You have the right to appeal this determination through our internal appeals process. To file a standard appeal, send a written request and supporting medical records within 180 days of the date of this notice. Include your member identification number (W102288591) and reference PA-2026-143614. You may also have the right to receive, upon request and without charge, copies of documents relevant to this determination.

Please note that benefits are subject to all terms, exclusions, and limitations of your group health plan document. This letter constitutes the formal adverse determination for the service identified above.

Sincerely,

Aetna Medical Management
Utilization Management Department
clinical_context: The 65-year-old member has Focal epilepsy, drug-resistant (G40.019). He has been treated under commercial coverage with Lacosamide add-on therapy recommended as the next appropriate step. Breakthrough seizures on levetiracetam and lamotrigine; EEG focal temporal spikes. This directly contradicts the insurer's position that emergency retroactive auth bars coverage: the chart documents dates, doses, and objective findings that were submitted with the original request submitted in Q1 2026 but are not reflected in the denial letter's rationale. Treating clinicians note ongoing functional impairment and avoidable harm if his care is delayed further.

insurer_voice.reasoning: Letter reads as authentic Aetna correspondence with CPB and InterQual fingerprints and procedural tone.
